what_it_does: This file presents the high-level architecture of the Company as a typed compositional dataflow system: it defines node types, composition patterns, the addressing and reactive-dataflow runtime, the event-sourced record, the human-in-the-loop interface, escalation and inbox triage, the autonomy ratchet, design tensions, the minimal genetic kernel, the current fragmentary build state, and open design questions.
observations: The system is described as an invariant compositional graph: a small fixed set of node types composed infinitely, with the topology itself being the program.
Node taxonomy includes survey, interpret, reduce/shape/route, synthesise/judge, and gate nodes.
Structured outputs act as the type system, enabling type-directed composition where edges are typed schemas.
Address is the universal primitive for persistence, provenance, context loading, and the coherent record.
Variable resolution triggers execution: phases run when their input addresses are populated, producing a blackboard/stigmergic dataflow.
The system has twin substrates: code for deterministic nodes and context (prompts, schemas, principles) for AI nodes.
It claims homoiconicity: markdown description and executable/context substrate are the same source.
It uses CQRS/event-sourcing and insists on persisting decision trajectories, not just endpoints.
The interface is framed as the API of the most expensive node (Tim), with voice, twin/brain, and translator/decision-compiler organs.
An escalation ladder runs from ground truth to the explicit/implicit model of Tim to real Tim.
Inbox triage has three lanes: resolved-for-you, batched walkthroughs, and live escalations with COA-style packages.
The autonomy ratchet tapers Tim's involvement as the twin improves, but spikes in novel territory.
The genetic kernel is defined as resolution engine + machine-enforceable constitution + interface.
Tim's existing projects (Vi Chat type registry, Obsidian vault, Vi Memory graph, local AI stack, AppData job) are reframed as organs of one body.
Many components are listed as gestured-but-not-built, and several explicit open questions remain.
standouts: "The topology is the program" and the invariant-primitives/variant-compositions distinction.
Address-driven, presence-of-data reactive dataflow with blackboard/stigmergy coordination.
Twin substrates (code + context) and homoiconic markdown as the basis for recursive self-construction.
Interface as the expensive node's API, especially the twin's selective-prediction-with-reject-option pattern.
The genetic kernel framing: the minimal seed from which everything else grows internally.
conventions: Markdown frontmatter with type and title.
Heavy use of bolded key terms and hierarchical section headers.
Declarative/dataflow vocabulary (addresses, phases, variables, blackboard, stigmergy).
Mixes architectural principles, current-state inventory, and open questions in one document.
concerns: A large portion of the architecture is aspirational or gestured rather than built, which may obscure what is currently real.
Several unresolved open questions are left in the document, including boundaries with TIM.md.
Dense neologisms and project-specific names may reduce readability for anyone outside the immediate context.
The claim that roughly a third is assembled is not substantiated within the file.
notes: A visionary architecture synthesis that reframes Tim's existing projects as organs of a single self-building, human-in-the-loop dataflow organism.
questions: What concrete schema/type language will the type registry use?
How will the resolution engine physically implement reactive dataflow and address resolution?
What is the exact boundary and relationship between this architecture file and TIM.md?
What corpus and training pipeline will produce the twin/model of Tim?
What are the initial structured-output schemas and which nodes consume them?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is an architecture synthesis, and the file does provide that vision plus an inventory and open questions; it also reads as a manifesto, which is consistent but broader.
apparent_themes: Deterministic-first design: spend AI only on irreducibly subjective steps.
Subsidiarity: push decisions to the cheapest competent layer.
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to unify Tim's scattered projects under one architectural narrative, define the irreducible kernel that lets the system grow itself, and specify how AI and human judgment are composed so Tim is consulted only on genuinely irreducible decisions.
novelty: The distinctive combination of typed compositional dataflow, address-driven blackboard/stigmergy, twin code-and-context substrates, homoiconic markdown, and a genetic-kernel framing for recursive self-growth.
contribution: A coherent architectural vocabulary and set of design laws—deterministic-first composition, type-directed edges, address-based reactive dataflow, escalation ladder, genetic kernel—that a fused system would keep as the conceptual scaffold for integrating deterministic code, AI nodes, context, and human gating.
summary_for_embedding: A synthesis document defining the Company as a typed, address-driven, event-sourced compositional dataflow system with a human-in-the-loop interface, twin substrates, and a minimal genetic kernel for recursive self-growth.
intention_for_embedding: To establish the unifying architecture and design laws for Tim's projects so the system can compose code and AI nodes, model Tim, and escalate only when irreducible.
